DISCOVERY, 302, AS TO PLAINTIFFS GAYLE MACCIA AND TILLMAN PEARCE'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES TO DEFENDANTS WAYNE WILLEY, PAMELA GOLDSMITH AND JOSHUA WILLEY; REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS AGAINST DEFENDANTS ATTORNEY JONATHAN BORNSTEIN IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,642, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR SANCTIONS IS GRANTED IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,642. DEFENDANT'S OPPOSITION WAS NOT FILED IN A TIMELY BASIS. IT APPEARS DEFENDANT PROVIDED RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES AS PART OF THE OPPOSITION PAPERS, BUT THESE ARE REFERRED TO AS FORM INTERROGATORY RESPONSES IN THE ACCOMPANYING DECLARATION. DEFENDANTS WAYNE WILLEY, JOSH WILLEY AND PAMELA GOLDSMITH MUST COMPLY WITH THIS ORDER BY SERVING THE DEATH CERTIFICATE OF MS. GOLDSMITH SO THAT PLAINTIFF MAY PROCEED WITH DISMISSAL AND PAYING THE SANCTIONS WITHIN 10 DAYS AFTER ENTRY OF THE ORDER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE PRO TEM: VEDICA PURI, CLERK: SEAN KANE, NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

Plaintiff Gayle Maccia, Tillman Pearce Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories To Defendants Wayne Willey, Pamela Goldsmith And Joshua Willey; Request For Sanctions Against Defendants Attorney Jonathan Bornstein In The Amount Of $1,642
Off Calendar Jan-28-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. MARY CATHERINE WIDERHOLD, ESQ., Tel No. 415-533-0735 appearing for the Plaintiffs;
No other appearances made in this matter.

Plaintiffs' counsel stipulates to the matter being heard by the pro tem judge. The Court adopts its tentative ruling with a modification. As to Plaintiffs Gayle Maccia and Tillman Pearce's Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories To Defendants Wayne Willey, Pamela Goldsmith And Joshua Willey; Request For Sanctions Against Defendants Attorney Jonathan Bornstein In The Amount Of $1,642, Plaintiff's motion for sanctions is GRANTED in the amount of $1,642. Defendant's Opposition was not filed in a timely basis. It appears Defendant provided responses to special interrogatories as part of the Opposition papers, but these are referred to as form interrogatory responses in the accompanying declaration. Defendants Wayne Willey, Josh Willey and Pamela Goldsmith must comply with this order by serving the death certificate of Ms. Goldsmith so that Plaintiff may proceed with dismissal and paying the sanctions within 10 days after entry of the order. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge Pro Tem: Vedica Puri, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/JPT)
